name: running the PR-quality eval gate twice replaces the prior result rather than appending duplicate entries
command: mkdir -p pr-corpus/grades && for i in $(seq 1 20); do printf '{"pr":%d,"graded_useful":true,"usefulness":4}\n' "$i" > pr-corpus/grades/p${i}.json; done && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/pr-corpus/quality-eval.sh" --grades-dir pr-corpus/grades --out pr-corpus/quality-eval.json --now 2026-05-13T00:00:00Z 2>/dev/null && bash "$CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT/pr-corpus/quality-eval.sh" --grades-dir pr-corpus/grades --out pr-corpus/quality-eval.json --now 2026-05-13T01:00:00Z 2>err.txt && grep -q '"computed_at":"2026-05-13T01:00:00Z"' pr-corpus/quality-eval.json && [ "$(grep -c computed_at pr-corpus/quality-eval.json)" = "1" ] && echo ok 1>&2
